description:
# fz-EL: Empty list ≠ nil

## The thing in one sentence

Today fz treats `nil` (keyword) and `[]` (empty-list literal) as the same
runtime value (both `SPECIAL_NIL = 0b011`), even though the type system
already wants them distinct. We split them: `[]` becomes a list-tagged
sentinel pointer (BEAM-style), `nil` stays as the special atom-like
value. The pattern-matcher bug on list_primitives disappears as a
consequence, spec types simplify (`nil | list(int)` → `list(int)`), and
the empty list gets a fast cheap representation needing no allocation.

## Background

`pattern_check` on `fixtures/list_primitives/input.fz` emits four bogus
warnings: every `fn` with `nil | cons` clauses is flagged as having one
unreachable arm + an inexhaustive last clause. Investigation traced
this to `pattern_matrix.rs` silently `continue`-ing on
`Pattern::List(vec![], None)` instead of routing it like `Pattern::Nil`.

But that's the surface. The deeper finding: the type lattice already
distinguishes `nil` (basic-bit) from the empty list (representable as
`list_of(none())`); the runtime conflation is the root. Patching the
matrix is a one-line band-aid that leaves the same bug class lurking
in every future pattern consumer. The elegant work is to fix the data
model.

## Concrete acceptance

  - `nil` and `[]` are distinct runtime values:
      nil: FzValue(0b011)            unchanged
      []:  FzValue(0x8)              new — TAG_PTR with a sentinel
  - `print(nil)` shows "nil"; `print([])` shows "[]".
  - The `nil | list(int)` artifact disappears from inferred spec types
    everywhere; signatures simplify to `list(int)`.
  - `pattern_check` on `list_primitives` and `sum_list`-shape fns no
    longer emits spurious warnings.
  - No `expected.txt` changes anywhere in the fixture matrix — observable
    program behavior preserved.

## Out of scope

  - Making `nil` a regular atom (`:nil`). Stays as `SPECIAL_NIL`. Future
    ticket if we want fuller BEAM conformance.
  - DPS / empty-list-as-zero-allocation perf measurement. Free side-
    effect of this work; pinning it is a different ticket.

## DAG

```
EL.1  IR scaffold: new variants/rename, runtime bits unchanged
        ↓
EL.2  Runtime bit-pattern split (the foundational behavior change)
        ↓
EL.3  Typer pattern-narrowing simplification
        ↓
EL.4  Sweep + cleanup
```

Strictly linear. See child tickets for details.

## Removals are first-class

  - EL.1: pattern_matrix.rs's "[| tail] degenerate continue" branch.
  - EL.1: the implicit "Pattern::Nil and Pattern::List([],None) are
    interchangeable" assumption baked into multiple consumers.
  - EL.2: NIL_BITS as the empty-list representation.
  - EL.3: spurious `nil |` artifacts in spec types.
  - EL.4: conflation-era comments ("contains nil"); any dead Pattern::Nil
    OR Pattern::List([]) branches.

## Process discipline

This epic gets its own worktree from origin/main, separate from any
in-flight branch. One ticket per commit. Goldens re-bless in the same
commit that earns the change.
title: [EPIC] Empty list ≠ nil: split runtime values, simplify type system
